source_file: 4-5.FL.2 Lesson - How Can SMART Goals Help You Achieve Your Dreams_.docx
output_file: 4-5.FL.2 Lesson - How Can SMART Goals Help You Achieve Your Dreams_.md

analysis.summary: This is Lesson 2 of a Financial Literacy curriculum for grades 4-5 that teaches students how to use the SMART goal framework (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) to set and achieve personal and financial goals. The lesson includes video content, handouts, progress tracking tools, and family engagement strategies to help students develop goal-setting skills.

entities.name: SMART Goals Framework
entities.description: Goal-setting framework using Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound criteria

entities.name: Financial Literacy Grades 4-5
entities.description: Elementary financial literacy curriculum for 4th and 5th grade students

entities.name: SS.5.35
entities.description: Nevada State Standard describing the importance of setting financial goals

entities.name: Saving 4-2a
entities.description: National standard for mapping out savings plan for future purchase objective

entities.name: Spending 4-1b
entities.description: National standard for brainstorming personal list of consumption goals

entities.name: 4-5.FL.2 Handout B SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning
entities.description: Student handout for brainstorming and planning SMART goals

entities.description: Student tool for monitoring progress toward SMART goals

entities.name: 4-5.FL.2 Anchor Chart SMART Goals and Achieving Your Dreams
entities.description: Visual reference chart for SMART goals concepts

entities.name: Green Our Planet Studios Video
entities.description: Educational video introducing SMART goals through character Quasar

entities.name: Goal Ladder Activity
entities.description: Visual activity breaking long-term goals into manageable steps

entities.name: Specific Goals
entities.description: Clearly defined goals that explain exactly what you're trying to achieve

entities.name: Measurable Goals
entities.description: Goals that can be tracked with numbers to monitor progress

entities.name: Achievable Goals
entities.description: Realistic goals that can actually be accomplished

entities.name: Relevant Goals
entities.description: Goals connected to something important you want to accomplish

entities.name: Time-bound Goals
entities.description: Goals with specific deadlines or timeframes for completion

entities.name: Self-Awareness SEL Competency
entities.description: Social-emotional learning skill for reflecting on personal aspirations and strengths

entities.name: Self-Management SEL Competency
entities.description: Social-emotional learning skill for organizing and tracking goal progress

entities.name: Family Engagement Strategies
entities.description: Methods for involving families in supporting student goal achievement

entities.name: Environmental Impact STEM Activity
entities.description: Independent practice connecting SMART goals to environmental conservation

relationships.context: This lesson addresses Nevada State Standard SS.5.35 about setting financial goals

Specific is the first component of the SMART framework
Measurable is a key component of SMART goals
Achievable ensures goals are realistic within SMART framework
Relevant connects goals to personal importance in SMART framework
Time-bound provides deadlines within SMART framework
